(or ambassadors) and remarried young widows; in the west, to

A calm sky, glossy and bright stars, breeze blowing in a
clock-wise direction, and the blaze in the horizon with the colour
of gold, are for the prosperity of mankind and kings.
[This verse, noted for its naturalness and simplicity, proves
that the author was an in-born poet worthy of being ranked with
Kalidasa. This reminds us of the latter's verse in the
Canto 1-23.
The metre is ^g^TT.]

Chapter XXXI I—Signs of Earthquake
